title: Ruf debuts new flat-eight engine at Goodwood
gist: German manufacturer Ruf debuted a new 4.8L flat-eight engine (the B8) producing over 1,000 hp at Goodwood, marking its evolution from Porsche tuner to independent carmaker building its own engines and chassis.

insights.claim: Ruf has evolved from a Porsche tuner into a distinct manufacturer, recognized by German authorities as such since the 1983 BTR carried Ruf's own VIN, and now building its own all-carbon monocoque chassis.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: A low-volume specialist moving up the value chain to in-house engines and chassis is a niche premium-brand independence play.
insights.locator: Ruf has come quite a long way from its roots

insights.claim: The new B8 engine is a 4.8L flat-eight generating more than 1,000 hp (745 kW) and 737 lb-ft (1,000 Nm), departing from the horizontally opposed six-cylinders used in prior Ruf cars like the SCR and Rodeo.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: A bespoke high-output engine signals Ruf's intent to differentiate its next generation on unique powertrain hardware rather than Porsche-derived engines.
insights.locator: Full technical specs on the new engine are not available yet
